_doc: Roster of PUBLIC-repo protocols the audit-watch loop sweeps on a cron. Each must have a live/standing bug-bounty program (find+report scope) — the loop AUDITS the code (no account needed); the operator confirms current live scope + submits. The real edge is DIFF-AWARENESS: re-audited on every HEAD change, so freshly-introduced bugs surface fast (most static audited code reads clean — see Morpho). Keep the roster honest: only protocols you can verify have an active program. Add newer/smaller protocols (better odds, less crowd) as you confirm them.
_honest: These are big, heavily-audited protocols → most sweeps will be clean (0 high). That is correct, not failure. Their value here is (1) proving the loop runs end-to-end on real code and (2) catching the moment NEW/changed contracts land. The bounty-likely targets are newer/smaller code — add those repos as the operator confirms live scope.
_addInstructions: Append { id, name, repo, sub?, program, programUrl, note }. sub = monorepo subdir holding the in-scope .sol. The loop shallow-clones repo, audits in-scope .sol, and cards only HIGH-severity NEW leads.

targets.name: Morpho Blue
targets.note: Lending primitive. Tiny core (~24 contracts), modern 0.8.x. Callback-pattern triggers benign reentrancy mediums — known-safe; only NEW high leads card.

targets.name: Euler Vault Kit
targets.note: Lending = rich surface (oracle, liquidation, interest math). Public EVK. Euler runs a large standing bounty.

targets.name: Sablier Lockup
targets.note: Token streaming. Self-contained 0.8.x. Confirm live bounty terms in SECURITY.md before submitting.
